paper_title: Sediment Microfacies Analysis and Varve Counting in Lake Chatyr Kol

methods.method_name: Sediment Microfacies Analysis and Varve Counting
methods.description: This method involves the preparation and analysis of sediment slabs to evaluate microfacies and count varves, which are used to establish a floating varve chronology. The process includes preparing thin sections, analyzing microfacies, measuring varve thickness, and assessing varve quality.

steps.description: Continuous 10-cm-long sediment slabs with an overlap of 2 cm were taken from the whole composite profile to prepare large-scale petrographic thin sections.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment slabs for thin section analysis

steps.description: Thin section preparation followed the method described by Brauer and Casanova (2001) and included freeze-drying and vacuum impregnation of the sediment slabs with Araldite epoxy resin.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze-dry and impregnate sediment slabs with epoxy resin

steps.description: Microfacies analysis, including a semi-quantitative evaluation of planktic and periphytic diatoms, aquatic plant remains, ostracods, daphnia, characeae and chrysophytes, was carried out on a Zeiss Axioplan microscope using different magnifications (25-400 x).
steps.searchable_summary: perform microfacies analysis using a microscope

steps.description: Measurement of varve thicknesses, microfacies/varve type characterization, the definition of varve boundaries and the development of process-related deposition models.
steps.searchable_summary: measure varve thickness and characterize varve types

steps.description: A varve quality index (VQI) ranging from 0-5 was given for each varve, comparable to the method from Żarczyński et al., 2018.
steps.searchable_summary: assign varve quality index to each varve

steps.description: Varve counting was performed to establish a floating varve chronology. Non-varved intervals (VQI=0) between varved sediment sections were interpolated by using the mean of sedimentation rates derived from about 20 varves above and below the non-varved part.
steps.searchable_summary: count varves and interpolate non-varved intervals

steps.description: Varves were counted twice by the same author. Counting uncertainty estimates were first assessed by the percentage deviation of the second to the first count within one thin section.
steps.searchable_summary: assess counting uncertainty by comparing counts
